name: Reduce live dashboard refresh latency to 200 ms

description: baseline for live real-data recalc target using tmp/autoresearch.runtime.html and full unit-test checks; median live /recalc is ~5.44 s with Codex and PI collection dominating
perf: cache codex session parses by file metadata

perf: cache unchanged provider log parses

Purpose: Reduce repeated live /recalc latency on real local data by reusing parsed results for unchanged Codex, Claude, and PI log files in the long-lived server process.

Includes:
- add live-data autoresearch harness and checks writing runtime HTML to tmp/
- cache Codex session parse results by file size and mtime
- cache PI session message records by file size and mtime
- cache Claude request records by file size and mtime
- update autoresearch notes and ideas backlog

name: Reduce live dashboard cold-start recalc latency

description: perf: baseline cold-start recalc latency after warm-cache wins

name: Reduce live dashboard restart cold-start recalc latency

description: perf: persist provider parse caches across restarts
perf: cache cold-start provider contributions more aggressively
perf: reuse file stat results from directory iteration to avoid redundant stat calls; warm improved slightly but cold median regressed versus 65.44 ms best
perf: lazily export dashboard_core package symbols to cut import overhead; import time dropped materially but cold median still regressed slightly versus 65.44 ms best
perf: cache priced codex file contributions for cold-start rebuilds
perf: cache priced Claude file contributions for cold-start rebuilds; extra serialized contribution shape increased cold latency and regressed well past the 60.63 ms best
perf: cache priced PI file contributions for cold-start rebuilds; warm improved slightly but cold median remained worse than the 60.63 ms best
perf: switch persistent cache serialization to pickle-backed binary format; benchmark crashed because the pickle import was missing
perf: switch persistent cache serialization to pickle-backed binary format; fresh-process benchmark crashed because the persistent cache magic constant was missing
perf: switch persistent cache serialization to pickle-backed binary format; restart cold latency stayed worse than the 60.63 ms best despite a smaller faster-loading cache blob
perf: cache Codex aggregate daily/activity totals across restarts; warm improved slightly but cold median regressed versus the 60.63 ms best

Measured current live-data restart-cold benchmark while investigating the user's slow browser refresh report; current daemon-side recalc remains fast (~67.1 ms cold, ~45.5 ms warm), so the intermittent multi-second refresh is not reproduced in the benchmark.

baseline: benchmark PI append-triggered cache invalidation by appending valid usage events to a temp copy of the 925 MiB live PI session; each append currently forces a full reparse at ~3381 ms median

perf: resume PI session parsing from the last verified byte offset when a log grows append-only; store verified head/boundary signatures plus parser state in the persistent PI cache so appends to the 925 MiB session no longer trigger a full reparse (~3380.9 ms -> ~0.36 ms median)

name: Parallel provider collection for live dashboard recalc

description: baseline: current sequential provider collection after timing diagnostics work; cold live recalc is ~134.2 ms median and warm is ~55.8 ms before testing provider-level parallelism

perf: collect Codex, Claude, and PI usage in parallel after loading persistent caches and before saving them; cold live recalc improved from ~134.2 ms to ~70.1 ms median while warm also edged down to ~52.4 ms
Attempted a screenshot-information baseline, but the autoresearch harness requires the repository entrypoint.

asi.hypothesis: The existing 96-column tmux output covers every screenshot field except the five-hour quota.
asi.rollback_reason: The experiment command was rejected before execution because autoresearch.sh is mandatory.
asi.next_action_hint: Use the repository autoresearch entrypoint for benchmark runs and validate tmux field coverage through targeted QA outside run_experiment.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description: Establish cold-recalc baseline before the tmux quota correctness fix.

asi.hypothesis: Capture the current cold and warm recalc latency so the quota-display fix can be checked for regressions.

description: tmux: preserve inactive five-hour quota without recalc regressions

asi.hypothesis: Reconcile a backend-omitted inactive five-hour quota and add an all-information compact render tier without affecting the recalc pipeline.
asi.rollback_reason: The unrelated cold-recalc median was 0.136 ms slower than baseline, within likely noise but not a primary-metric improvement.
asi.next_action_hint: Reapply the correctness patch unchanged and rerun to distinguish benchmark noise from a real regression; the tmux code is outside the measured recalc path.
asi.correctness_evidence: Targeted live output contained 5h, weekly reset, Today, MTD, cost, and refresh interval within 96 columns; 41 tests passed.

description:
tmux: Preserve inactive five-hour quota

Purpose: Keep the five-hour ChatGPT quota visible when Codex omits a fully reset inactive window.

Includes:
- reconcile standard five-hour and weekly windows without replacing active quota observations
- prefer compact quota labels before dropping Today, MTD, cost, or refresh timing
- invalidate legacy quota snapshots and document the fallback behavior
- validate live output and all 41 unit tests

asi.hypothesis: The quota reconciliation and compact render tier restore the missing five-hour information while leaving the measured recalc path unchanged.
asi.correctness_evidence: Live output rendered 5h 100%, 7d 91% with reset, Today 77.4M, MTD 1.07B, $857, and 11:57 to 12:00 within 96 columns; full backpressure checks passed.
asi.performance_interpretation: The 0.846 ms cold improvement is likely benchmark noise because the changed tmux modules are outside the recalc path; the important result is no observed regression.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Splice dynamic dashboard regions in one pass

Purpose: Reduce cold and warm recalc latency by replacing repeated full-HTML regex rewrites with one ID-anchored splice pass.

Includes:
- locate the five dynamic dashboard elements by stable IDs
- rebuild the HTML once for the common path while retaining regex compatibility fallbacks
- preserve byte-for-byte output equivalence for the live template
- validate all 41 unit tests and autoresearch backpressure checks

asi.hypothesis: Repeated DOTALL regex substitutions and full-string copies account for much of the remaining render cost; one ID-anchored splice pass should reduce real recalc latency without skipping any rendering or collection work.
asi.correctness_evidence: The fast path produced byte-for-byte identical output to the prior regex pipeline on the live runtime HTML; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 53.529 ms best to 46.588 ms (13.0%), while warm median improved from 41.389 ms to 33.084 ms (20.1%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: The change performs every existing dynamic replacement and retains the original regex fallback for legacy templates; no data collection, parsing, or payload work was skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Deserialize persistent cache entries in place

Purpose: Reduce cold-start cache hydration overhead without changing cache validity or provider parsing behavior.

Includes:
- reuse cache-owned dictionaries produced by JSON decoding instead of cloning each record
- preserve serialized timezone offsets without redundant local timezone conversion
- retain the existing cache format, signatures, and invalidation rules
- validate timestamp equivalence, all 41 unit tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Fresh JSON decoding already owns every cache object, so cloning thousands of records and converting already-local timestamp offsets is redundant cold-start work.
asi.correctness_evidence: All 3,642 cached timestamps matched the prior local ISO representation exactly; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 46.588 ms best to 45.132 ms (3.1%). Warm moved from 33.084 ms to 35.781 ms, likely run noise because cache hydration is bypassed after the first in-process load; it remains far below the 200 ms target.
asi.benchmark_integrity: The persistent cache format, file signatures, invalidation checks, provider scans, parsing, aggregation, and rendered payload remain unchanged; only redundant cache object reconstruction was removed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Prune deleted files from persistent caches

Purpose: Reduce cold cache loading by removing records whose source files no longer exist under each fully scanned provider root.

Includes:
- track live cache keys during complete Codex, Claude, and PI scans
- prune only stale entries belonging to the scanned provider root
- shrink the live cache from 1.52 MB to 0.79 MB
- validate root isolation, all 41 unit tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Persistent cache entries for deleted sessions are loaded and deserialized forever; pruning them after the existing complete provider scans should reduce cold starts without excluding any live source file.
asi.correctness_evidence: The live cache retained all 706 Codex, 40 Claude, 40 Claude-attribution, and 134 PI files while shrinking from 1,523,484 to 791,650 bytes; root-isolation QA, all 41 tests, and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 45.132 ms best to 43.064 ms (4.6%). Warm moved from 35.781 ms to 37.068 ms; the small live-key tracking overhead remains far below the 200 ms target and is secondary to the cold improvement.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every provider root is still fully enumerated and every live file retains the same stat, invalidation, parse, aggregation, and rendering path; only cache entries for absent files are removed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Reuse directory-validated session file indexes

Purpose: Avoid repeated directory enumeration while preserving complete discovery and per-file invalidation on cold and warm recalculations.

Includes:
- persist each provider root's ordered JSONL file list and directory mtimes
- reuse the list only after validating every scanned directory's mtime
- trigger a stable full rescan whenever a directory changes or disappears
- validate ordering, add/delete/content-change behavior, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Provider session file names change far less often than file contents, so a persisted list can avoid repeated directory scans when every previously scanned directory mtime is unchanged; existing per-file size and mtime checks still detect content changes.
asi.correctness_evidence: The index preserved exact legacy traversal order on all live roots, reused safely after content-only changes, rebuilt after file additions and deletions, survived persistence reload, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 43.064 ms best to 34.950 ms (18.8%), while warm median improved from 37.068 ms to 29.097 ms (21.5%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every indexed directory is stat-validated before reuse and every listed file still receives its existing size/mtime validation; any directory metadata change forces the original complete scan, so no source discovery or parsing work is silently skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Keep indexed session paths as strings

Purpose: Remove repeated Path construction from unchanged-file cache hits while preserving the same signature and parsing behavior.

Includes:
- yield persisted file-index paths directly as strings
- use os.stat and os.fspath for cache lookup and validation
- construct Path objects only when a changed file requires parsing
- validate string and Path equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: The persisted file index already contains normalized path strings, so rebuilding hundreds of Path objects on every scan is redundant; unchanged cache hits can stay string-native and convert only on a real parse.
asi.correctness_evidence: Codex, Claude request, Claude attribution, and PI cache hits returned identical results for string and Path inputs; all 41 unit tests and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 34.950 ms best to 30.981 ms (11.4%), while warm median improved from 29.097 ms to 24.667 ms (15.2%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every indexed path still receives os.stat size/mtime validation, and changed or append-growing files are converted to Path before the unchanged parser and signature logic runs; no file or parsing work is skipped.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Evaluate session ID path fallbacks lazily

Purpose: Avoid computing file stems for valid cached session IDs while preserving malformed-record fallback behavior.

Includes:
- normalize cached session IDs before evaluating path-derived fallbacks
- defer PI path stems on valid stat and cache-hit paths
- preserve fallback IDs for blank or missing session metadata
- validate lazy behavior, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: All live cached records contain session IDs, but path-derived fallbacks are evaluated eagerly; making the fallback lazy should remove unnecessary basename work without changing malformed-record handling.
asi.correctness_evidence: All live providers completed with path-stem evaluation configured to fail, proving valid cache records never need the fallback; explicit blank-ID fallback QA, all 41 tests, and backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median moved from 30.981 ms to 30.962 ms (0.06% improvement), which is far inside observed run noise; warm moved to 25.452 ms. A confirmation run is required before treating this as a real keep.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every source file, signature check, cache lookup, parse, and aggregation remains unchanged; only evaluation timing of an existing fallback was changed.

description: perf: discard lazy session ID path fallback after confirmation

asi.hypothesis: Reconfirm whether deferring path-stem fallbacks for valid cached session IDs produces a measurable cold-start improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: The confirmation cold median regressed to 31.908 ms versus the persisted 30.981 ms best, showing the initial 0.02 ms apparent gain was benchmark noise.
asi.next_action_hint: Return to higher-leverage work such as reducing aggregation round operations or compacting the remaining 0.8 MB JSON cache; do not retry this micro-optimization.
asi.correctness_evidence: The implementation remained correct across lazy-fallback QA, 41 tests, and backpressure checks, but did not improve the primary metric reliably.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Compact Codex contributions into positional cache entries

Purpose: Reduce cold cache decoding and Codex aggregation overhead without changing contribution values, signatures, or pricing behavior.

Includes:
- migrate legacy Codex cache dictionaries to backward-compatible positional entries
- unpack normalized contribution tuples directly in the Codex aggregation loop
- reduce the Codex cache section from 386 KB to 229 KB
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Repeated Codex cache field names and dictionary lookups add avoidable JSON size and aggregation overhead; positional entries can preserve identical values while reducing both.
asi.correctness_evidence: Legacy-to-positional round trips preserved every contribution, the compact cache reloaded all 706 entries, the full live payload matched HEAD byte-for-byte at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 30.981 ms best to 29.936 ms (3.4%). Warm held effectively flat at 24.674 ms versus 24.667 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: All file indexes, stat signatures, pricing keys, contributions, parsing fallbacks, aggregation, and rendered payloads remain intact; only the internal persisted representation and cache-hit access shape changed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description: perf: discard integer-nanodollar rounding fast path

asi.hypothesis: Most aggregation inputs are already rounded to nanodollars, so a conservative integer fast path far from rounding boundaries should reduce repeated Python round calls while preserving exact values.
asi.rollback_reason: Cold median regressed from the persisted 29.936 ms best to 31.489 ms despite exact output equivalence; branch checks and extra arithmetic outweighed any faster rounded-value cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not revisit round_cost micro-optimizations; focus on data-shape reductions such as compact Claude/PI cache entries or larger aggregation changes with measurable leverage.
asi.correctness_evidence: The implementation matched Python rounding across 1,500,003 randomized and boundary-focused values, produced an exact live payload match, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Batch Codex aggregation before cost rounding

Purpose: Reduce repeated model updates and nanodollar rounding while preserving complete per-session Codex aggregation.

Includes:
- accumulate every Codex contribution directly into daily, model, and activity buckets
- round each completed cost bucket once instead of after every contribution
- preserve unique session counts, token totals, pricing completeness, and activity hours
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Codex contributions are already individually priced to nanodollar precision, so accumulating every contribution before final bucket rounding should preserve output while removing thousands of repeated method and round calls.
asi.correctness_evidence: The batched collector produced an exact full live payload match against HEAD at a fixed clock, retained all session/model/activity semantics, and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 29.936 ms best to 28.666 ms (4.2%), while warm median improved from 24.674 ms to 22.209 ms (10.0%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Codex file is still discovered, stat-validated, parsed or cache-read, priced, and included; only the timing of mathematically equivalent bucket accumulation and final nine-decimal rounding changed.

name: Cold dashboard recalc after daemon restart

description:
perf: Batch Claude aggregation before cost rounding

Purpose: Reduce repeated daily and model-bucket rounding while preserving Claude request deduplication and attribution behavior.

Includes:
- accumulate every deduplicated Claude request directly into daily and model buckets
- round each completed cost bucket once after attribution processing
- preserve session activity, context attribution shares, pricing, and unique session counts
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Claude requests are individually priced before aggregation, so batching daily and model totals can remove thousands of repeated method and rounding calls while leaving deduplication, attribution, and activity semantics unchanged.
asi.correctness_evidence: The batched Claude collector produced an exact full live payload match against HEAD at a fixed clock and passed all 41 tests plus backpressure checks.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 28.666 ms best to 27.758 ms (3.2%). Warm median measured 23.401 ms versus 22.209 ms, with individual warm runs as low as 21.226 ms; the changed Claude path is faster in isolated profiling and the median shift appears to be run variance.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Claude file and request is still discovered, stat-validated, deduplicated, priced, attributed, and aggregated; only equivalent daily/model accumulation and final nine-decimal rounding timing changed.

description:
perf: Memoize pricing rate resolution per catalog

Purpose: Avoid repeatedly scanning immutable provider rate cards for every usage record of the same model.

Includes:
- cache resolved and unmapped rates by provider and model for each catalog instance
- preserve longest-prefix model matching, native-cost handling, warnings, and per-request pricing
- keep cache lifetime scoped to the loaded pricing catalog
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: A recalc prices hundreds of Claude requests but uses only a few provider/model combinations, so memoizing immutable rate resolution inside the per-recalc catalog should eliminate repeated rule scans and ModelRates construction without skipping any pricing work.
asi.correctness_evidence: Resolved and unmapped model rates memoized correctly, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from 27.758 ms to 26.021 ms (6.3%), and warm median improved from 23.401 ms to 20.659 ms (11.7%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every provider record is still discovered, validated, deduplicated, and priced independently; only immutable provider/model rate lookup results are reused within one PricingCatalog instance.

description:
perf: Compact Claude requests into positional cache entries

Purpose: Reduce cold cache decoding and repeated request-field normalization without changing Claude deduplication or pricing semantics.

Includes:
- migrate legacy Claude request dictionaries to backward-compatible positional entries
- carry validated request tuples through cross-file request deduplication
- preserve timestamp and token maxima, model selection, session fallback, and pricing
- shrink the persistent cache by about 82 KB and validate exact live payload equivalence

asi.hypothesis: Claude request records repeat nine field names hundreds of times and are revalidated after cache hydration; positional typed entries should reduce JSON decoding, object creation, and collector work while preserving every request.
asi.correctness_evidence: Legacy and compact cache formats both hydrate to the same validated records, all request aggregation tests passed, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 26.021 ms best to 25.508 ms (2.0%), while warm median improved slightly from 20.659 ms to 20.488 ms. The cache shrank from 746,678 to 664,800 bytes and isolated Claude collection fell from about 4.36 ms to 3.81 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: All Claude files are still discovered and stat-validated, every request remains globally deduplicated with identical max-token and latest-timestamp rules, and every resulting request is independently priced; only the internal cache and dedupe representation changed.

description:
perf: Collect optimized providers sequentially

Purpose: Remove thread scheduling and GIL contention now that cache-hit provider aggregation is short and CPU-bound.

Includes:
- collect Codex, Claude, and PI in deterministic sequence with the shared pricing catalog
- retain per-provider and overall collection timing diagnostics
- preserve complete provider scans, cache validation, aggregation, and output ordering
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: After cache and aggregation optimizations, provider collectors are short Python-heavy phases whose threaded execution contends on the GIL; sequential execution should now be faster than ThreadPoolExecutor scheduling while doing identical work.
asi.correctness_evidence: A 40-round paired live comparison produced identical provider totals and measured 9.374 ms sequential versus 10.448 ms threaded medians; the complete fixed-clock live payload matched HEAD exactly, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 25.508 ms best to 24.151 ms (5.3%), and warm median improved from 20.488 ms to 18.725 ms (8.6%).
asi.benchmark_integrity: Codex, Claude, and PI are still fully discovered, stat-validated, parsed or cache-read, priced, and aggregated on every recalc; only their scheduling changed from contended worker threads to deterministic sequential execution.
asi.tradeoff_note: Provider parallelism was valuable before the collectors were compressed and batched, but paired measurements on the current complete live workload show its scheduling and GIL costs now exceed any I/O overlap.

description:
perf: Batch PI aggregation before cost rounding

Purpose: Reduce repeated PI model, activity, and nanodollar updates while preserving native-cost and session semantics.

Includes:
- accumulate PI usage directly into typed daily, model, and per-session activity buckets
- merge session activity before rounding completed global buckets once
- preserve native-cost pricing, unique sessions, activity hours, and pricing completeness
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: PI usage rows are individually priced before aggregation, so typed bucket accumulation and one final rounding pass should remove repeated dictionary normalization and thousands of intermediate rounds without changing results.
asi.correctness_evidence: A standalone batched implementation matched the current PI daily and activity totals exactly, the production full live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved slightly from the persisted 24.151 ms best to 24.049 ms (0.4%), and warm median improved from 18.725 ms to 18.603 ms. Isolated PI collection improved more clearly from about 2.43 ms to 1.92 ms (21%), so the small end-to-end delta is consistent with the optimization being partly masked by run variance.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every PI file and contribution is still discovered, stat-validated, parsed or cache-read, natively priced, and included; only equivalent typed accumulation and final nine-decimal rounding timing changed.

description:
perf: Skip unused YTD row materialization

Purpose: Avoid serializing and sorting YTD artifacts that the pipeline never reads while preserving every dashboard output.

Includes:
- make daily row and ranked-value materialization independently optional with unchanged defaults
- skip provider YTD rows and rankings when only summaries are consumed
- retain combined YTD rankings and aggregate breakdowns used by the dashboard tables
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: The four YTD passes build nested per-day JSON rows that are discarded immediately; selectively materializing only consumed summaries, combined rankings, and aggregate breakdowns should remove duplicate serialization without omitting output.
asi.correctness_evidence: YTD materialization time fell from roughly 1.21 ms to 0.65 ms in stable fresh-process diagnostics, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 24.049 ms best to 23.638 ms (1.7%). Warm median was effectively flat at 18.639 ms versus 18.603 ms, within run variance.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every YTD date still contributes to provider and combined summaries, all combined days remain ranked for the table, and aggregate breakdowns remain complete; only internal row dictionaries and provider ranking lists proven unused by the pipeline are not constructed.

description:
perf: Compact Claude attribution into positional cache entries

Purpose: Reduce cold cache decoding and repeated attribution normalization without changing attribution shares or categories.

Includes:
- migrate legacy attribution dictionaries to backward-compatible positional events
- carry validated category, name, session, and timestamp tuples through grouping
- preserve event deduplication, session fallback, category counts, and proportional usage shares
- shrink the persistent cache by about 35 KB and validate exact live payload equivalence

asi.hypothesis: Claude attribution events repeat four field names hundreds of times and are normalized again during grouping; positional typed events should reduce cache decoding and collector overhead while preserving every event.
asi.correctness_evidence: Legacy and positional attribution formats hydrate to the same typed events, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 23.638 ms best to 23.145 ms (2.1%), and warm median improved from 18.639 ms to 16.968 ms (9.0%). The cache shrank from 664,793 to 629,743 bytes and isolated Claude collection improved from about 3.75 ms to 3.49 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Claude attribution file is still discovered and stat-validated, all events retain their category, name, session, and timestamp, and the same category-level proportional usage shares are computed; only the internal cache and grouping representation changed.

description:
perf: Price exact Claude request groups once

Purpose: Eliminate repeated pricing and bucket updates when grouped request pricing is mathematically identical.

Includes:
- group deduplicated Claude requests by date, session, and model before aggregation
- price a group once only when rates are integer nanodollars per token
- retain per-request pricing automatically for arbitrary custom rates
- preserve request maxima, tokens, sessions, attribution activity, warnings, and exact costs

asi.hypothesis: The live workload has 542 deduplicated Claude requests but only 18 date/session/model groups; for integer-nanodollar token rates, pricing complete groups once is exactly equivalent and removes hundreds of cost objects and bucket updates.
asi.correctness_evidence: Grouped and per-request costs matched across 8,000 randomized built-in-model cases, a non-integral custom rate correctly selected the per-request fallback, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 23.145 ms best to 21.075 ms (8.9%), and warm median improved from 16.968 ms to 14.674 ms (13.5%). Isolated Claude collection fell from about 3.49 ms to 1.01 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Claude request is still parsed or cache-read and globally deduplicated with identical maxima; all request tokens are grouped by the exact dimensions consumed downstream, and grouped pricing is used only when nanodollar additivity is proven, with a correctness-preserving fallback otherwise.

description: perf: skip unused all-time summary and ranking materialization; micro-timings improved but end-to-end cold and warm recalc regressed

asi.hypothesis: All-time materializations only feed browser rows, so skipping their unused summaries and rankings should remove thousands of rounded cost updates without changing output.
asi.correctness_evidence: All 41 tests and backpressure checks passed, stable diagnostics reduced all-time materialization from about 1.03 ms to 0.48 ms, and the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock.
asi.result_interpretation: Despite the isolated reduction, cold median regressed from the persisted 21.075 ms best to 22.047 ms (4.6%) and warm median regressed from 14.674 ms to 15.938 ms (8.6%).
asi.rollback_reason: The primary metric worsened materially and the secondary metric moved in the same direction, so the selective all-time materialization change is not retained.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not retry this internal materialization flag immediately; target measured cold-cache hydration or Codex cache-hit work with clearer end-to-end leverage.
asi.benchmark_integrity: The experiment retained every all-time browser row and changed only unused internal artifacts; no provider, date, or output field was omitted.

description:
perf: Hydrate compact cache entries directly

Purpose: Reduce restart cache loading by avoiding legacy normalization work for current positional entries.

Includes:
- hydrate compact Codex contributions and Claude request and attribution tuples directly
- retain defensive dictionary normalization for backward-compatible legacy cache entries
- preserve timestamp parsing, field fallbacks, file signatures, pricing keys, and contribution values
- validate legacy equivalence, exact live payloads, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Current positional cache entries were normalized before serialization, so routing them through legacy per-field normalization on every restart is redundant; direct hydration should reduce cold load while leaving legacy dictionaries supported.
asi.correctness_evidence: Compact and reconstructed legacy entries hydrated to identical typed values, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 21.075 ms best to 20.046 ms (4.9%). Warm median measured 14.855 ms versus 14.674 ms, a small 1.2% shift consistent with variance because this change only affects first-process cache hydration.
asi.benchmark_integrity: The same cache file and every provider entry are still decoded; all source files retain per-recalc stat validation and Codex pricing-key validation. Only already-normalized compact fields bypass legacy string stripping and integer clamping.

description:
perf: Compact PI contribution rows into typed entries

Purpose: Reduce PI cache decoding and repeated row normalization while preserving append-resume behavior.

Includes:
- serialize PI usage and activity rows as positional entries with legacy dictionary migration
- hydrate typed row tuples for the unchanged-file collection path
- convert tuples back to mutable indexed rows only while parsing appended events
- preserve native costs, signatures, offsets, session activity, and full-reparse equivalence

asi.hypothesis: PI cache rows repeat field names and perform dictionary validation on every recalc; typed positional rows should reduce disk size, cold hydration, and cache-hit collection while retaining mutable append parsing.
asi.correctness_evidence: Typed rows matched legacy live output exactly, append-resume matched a clean full parse, persistent serialization round-tripped exactly, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed under both system and development Python.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 20.046 ms best to 19.715 ms (1.7%), and warm median improved from 14.855 ms to 14.269 ms (3.9%). The cache shrank by about 25 KB, cold hydration improved by about 0.09 ms, and isolated PI collection improved from about 1.92 ms to 1.79 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every PI file and row is still discovered, stat-validated, natively priced, and aggregated. Append-only growth still validates boundary signatures and resumes from the prior offset; only the stable row representation changed.

description:
perf: Clone first provider totals without rounded merges

Purpose: Avoid empty-object merge overhead for provider buckets that do not overlap.

Includes:
- directly clone first-seen daily, breakdown, attribution, and activity totals
- retain rounded merge behavior only for true cross-provider overlaps
- preserve complete object independence between source and combined totals
- validate exact live payload equivalence, all 41 tests, and autoresearch checks

asi.hypothesis: Most combined keys belong to one provider, so cloning the first complete bucket directly should avoid redundant method calls and cost rounding while preserving independent combined objects.
asi.correctness_evidence: Direct clones matched existing combined totals exactly, source and combined daily/nested/activity objects remained independent, the complete live payload matched HEAD exactly at a fixed clock, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 19.715 ms best to 19.138 ms (2.9%), while warm median held effectively flat at 14.215 ms versus 14.269 ms. Isolated daily plus activity combination improved from about 1.56 ms to 0.69 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every provider daily, breakdown, attribution, and activity bucket is still copied into an independent combined structure; all overlapping keys still use the original rounded merge semantics. No provider data or output row is omitted.

description:
perf: Aggregate Codex costs as integer nanodollars

Purpose: Remove repeated floating-point rounding from cached Codex contribution aggregation.

Includes:
- persist explicitly marked integer-nanodollar Codex cost fields with legacy float migration
- accumulate daily, model, and activity costs as exact integers
- convert completed buckets back to USD before shared aggregation and rendering
- preserve pricing invalidation, sessions, tokens, cost completeness, and exact payload values

asi.hypothesis: Cached Codex costs are already rounded to nine decimals, so integer nanodollar accumulation can replace thousands of float-round calls while preserving exact values for built-in and custom pricing.
asi.correctness_evidence: 200,000 randomized nine-decimal costs round-tripped exactly through nanodollars, the migrated live cache produced a byte-identical fixed-clock payload versus HEAD, and all 41 tests plus backpressure checks passed under system and development Python.
asi.result_interpretation: Cold median improved from the persisted 19.138 ms best to 18.880 ms (1.3%), and warm median improved from 14.215 ms to 13.380 ms (5.9%). Isolated Codex collection improved from about 3.46 ms to 2.84 ms.
asi.benchmark_integrity: Every Codex contribution is still discovered, stat-validated, pricing-key validated, and aggregated. Costs retain full nine-decimal precision and are converted back to USD before any cross-provider logic or output generation.
asi.tradeoff_note: The explicit format marker and integer costs increase the cache by about 5 KB, offset by lower aggregation CPU.
